danger_signals.visual_form: Animated light beams scanning the street in side-scroller sections
danger_signals.meaning: Active enemy detection; immediate threat if operative is caught in beam

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark figure outlines visible through embassy windows during sniper scope view
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy positions; targets for sniper fire to clear operative descent routes

danger_signals.visual_form: Projectiles and muzzle flashes from terrorists during hallway/room sections
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat engagement; operative health threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Map markers indicating terrorist locations within embassy floors
danger_signals.meaning: Proximity danger; alerts player to incoming threats in adjacent rooms

safety_signals.visual_form: Buildings marked as sniper positions across from embassy (in side-scroller layout)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for sniper operatives; progression indicator

safety_signals.visual_form: Fences and building structures in street traversal sections
safety_signals.meaning: Shelter from searchlight detection and terrorist fire

safety_signals.visual_form: Hostages following operative character in third-person view; visual grouping
safety_signals.meaning: Mission objective progress; civilians in protective state

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated secure locations within embassy (in some port versions)
safety_signals.meaning: Final hostage containment; mission completion staging area

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Military operatives with tactical equipment (snipers, submachine guns, rappelling gear)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct from terrorists through equipment and positioning (external vantage points, helicopter insertion)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sniper nests in buildings across street; helicopter insertion point at embassy roof

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Armed figures within embassy; control searchlight apparatus; take hostages
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visible as dark silhouettes in windows; armed figures in hallways and rooms; searchlight operators
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Interior embassy positions; searchlight placement on perimeter; hostage holding areas within building

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Civilian figures requiring rescue; distinct character model from operatives and terrorists
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unarmed; follow operative when rescued; must be protected from crossfire
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Located throughout embassy interior; some designated safe rooms for containment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Street side-scroller view with searchlight sweeps, buildings with vantage point entries, quiet embassy exterior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Operatives move cautiously, avoid searchlights through timing and cover; no active gunfire; stealth-focused progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Split-screen or alternating scope view showing embassy windows with terrorist silhouettes; sniper crosshair targeting interface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Snipers shoot to clear windows; rappelling operative descends alongside building; synchronized multi-operative action

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person hallway perspective with doors and intersections; third-person room views showing terrorist positions, hostages, environmental detail
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Active combat; operative searches rooms systematically; hostages follow when rescued; snipers support via window fire if visible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Newspaper-style report display detailing operation results and casualty counts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No player control; automatic evaluation of speed, accuracy, hostages rescued, operative safety; generates quality-tiered success messaging

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Positive newspaper headline; celebratory language in report
Critical or qualified newspaper language; casualty details in report
Negative headline; condemnatory report tone; mission failure text

environmental_storytelling.example: Embassy building design reveals sniper lines of sight, terrorist positions in windows, hostage locations through floor-by-floor traversal

environmental_storytelling.technique: Searchlight placement as defensive posture indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Street-side searchlights demonstrate terrorist awareness of external threat; their coverage patterns show which approaches are fortified
Ending newspaper reports grade mission success by hostage/operative survival rates, implying diplomatic and PR fallout from casualties

environmental_storytelling.technique: Operative entry method as mission phase clarity
environmental_storytelling.example: Three distinct gameplay sections (sniper setup, external support, interior assault) visually separate tactical stages of counterterrorism operation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Hostage visual state as objective status
environmental_storytelling.example: Hostages follow operative after rescue, visually confirming mission progress; grouped hostages in safe rooms show containment success

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Automated sweeping motion across street; triggers if operative detected within beam
Silhouette visible but stationary; becomes actionable target for sniper fire
Opens fire on operative; requires evasion, cover use, or return fire
Follows operative character; must be guided to safe room or extraction point
Takes scope view; fires to clear windows and support rappelling teammate descent
Lowers down embassy wall; can select entry floor/window based on sniper coverage of windows
Searches rooms systematically using map; encounters and defeats terrorists; protects rescued hostages from crossfire

notes: This tactical shooter communicates a structured counterterrorism operation through three distinct gameplay phases, each with specific visual and behavioral language. Danger is primarily signaled through active detection systems (searchlights), enemy silhouettes, and hostile fire. Safety manifests through spatial positioning (vantage points, cover, safe rooms) and objective completion (hostage acquisition). The faction division is clear: external military force vs. internal hostage-takers vs. protected civilians. World state transitions are marked by gameplay perspective shifts (side-scroller → scope view → first/third-person), creating a visual grammar of mission phases. The ending system uses newspaper-style reporting to communicate political/diplomatic consequences of casualty counts, implying that mission success is measured not just by tactical completion but by collateral damage minimization. NES version uses militarized difficulty names rather than numerical ranks, reinforcing military hierarchy framing.
